sectionOrderRules: Hero must communicate what the company manufactures, the main customer value and a primary CTA.
Product-category-summary must appear before the final contact CTA.
Trust proof must rely on real signals such as support approach, security information, delivery capability, references or documented experience when such data exists.
Applications-summary should connect products to real use cases rather than listing technical terms without context.
Sections must avoid repeating the same product claims in multiple blocks.
Products intro must explain the scope of the offering in plain language.
Product-category-list items must describe concrete product groups, key benefits and typical use cases.
If product-comparison-notes is shown, it should help visitors choose category without pretending to be a full specification sheet.
Contact CTA should invite quote, product dialogue or requirement discussion.
Applications intro must explain how the products are used in practice.
Application-list entries must describe industries, environments or workflows the products fit, not vague audience labels.
Case snippets may only be shown when the dossier contains real customer-safe examples or anonymised use cases.
The page should connect each application to relevant product groups without duplicating the full products page.
About story must describe the company in a specific and credible way.
Team should focus on relevant competence, roles or service responsibility when data exists.
Trust proof should highlight real capabilities such as support, quality routines, security work or customer relationships without inventing certifications.
News list should contain actual updates, articles or announcements when available.
If there are too few news items, the renderer should keep the page concise rather than padding with generic text.
Contact CTA should fit the context, for example product questions, press contact or partnership dialogue.
Contact info must include the real contact methods available from the business data.
If a form is shown, it should support product, sales or support enquiries.
Map should only render when a real visit or office address exists and is suitable to publish.
